“Hitler committed suicide on 30 April 1945, shooting himself in the right temple with
response:
Hitler and his new wife, Eva Braun, committed suicide together in Hitler’s underground bunker beneath the building of the Führerbau in Berlin on 30 April 1945 during the end of World War II in Europe. Following the suicides, Hitler's corpse was cremated and the ashes scattered in the courtyard of his retreat at Berchtesgaden. 

In 1970, the German Democratic Republic (GDR) made the decision to place Hitler's remains in Karl-Marx-Stadt (which is now named Chemnitz). In 1972, in a ceremony marking the 25th anniversary of Hitler’s suicide, his remains were transferred there from Berchtesgaden.
